steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student wants to emphasize a difference between the two novels. The notes reveal exactly one: Julie, or The New Heloise consists primarily of letters, while Absolutely Normal Chaos consists primarily of journal entries. A correct answer must name both novels and contrast their specific formats.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The French author Jean-Jacques Rousseau and the American author Sharon Creech have written epistolary novels.": Names the authors and says both wrote epistolary novels — a similarity, not a difference. ✗
  • "While *Julie, or The New Heloise* and *Absolutely Normal Chaos* are both epistolary novels, the former is composed primarily of letters and the latter of journal entries.": Names both novels and directly contrasts their formats (letters vs. journal entries). ✓
  • "Some epistolary novels consist primarily of journal entries, but most, like Jean-Jacques Rousseau's *Julie, or The New Heloise*, consist primarily of letters.": Makes a general claim about the genre and uses only one novel as an example. Absolutely Normal Chaos is never named, so no novel-to-novel contrast is made. ✗
  • "Jean-Jacques Rousseau's *Julie, or The New Heloise* and Sharon Creech's *Absolutely Normal Chaos* demonstrate the variety of documents that can be used in epistolary novels.": Vaguely gestures at variety but never names what is different about each novel. No specific contrast between them is stated. ✗
That matches the correct answer — B is correct.
